blocks.body: Sentence types (types of sentences) are the main categories of sentences according to the purpose of expression. There are four main types in the English language: affirmative, negative, interrogative, and imperative. In this topic, you will learn the four types of sentences in English: affirmative, negative, interrogative, and imperative. — their structure and features.

{ "title": "9. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "Affirmative: S + V + O — states a fact, ends with a period.", "Negative: S + do/does/did + not + V — negates an action.", "Interrogative (Interrogative): Do/Does/Did + S + V? — asks a question, ends with a question mark.", "Imperative: V + O — expresses an order or request, without a subject.", "Auxiliary verbs (do/does/did) are needed in negatives and questions.", "After does/did verb is used without ending -s.", "Abbreviated forms: don't, doesn't, isn't, aren't." ] }
